summary: No post-2024 paper resolving Conjecture 4.6 was found. The conjecture asks whether every polynomial growth rate $2^{n^c}$ is achievable as a lower bound for $r(K_s^{(3)}, K_n^{(3)})$ for some fixed $s$ depending on $c$. The closest follow-up work (arXiv:2411.13812) studies the complementary polynomial-growth regime for general $H$, and arXiv:2603.16069 achieves quasipolynomial rates for specific non-complete hypergraphs; neither directly addresses the complete-hypergraph setting of Conjecture 4.6. The sole internal reference (arXiv:2312.15572) concerns a different conjecture about VC-dimension and is unrelated.

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper is about induced subgraph density and VC-dimension (proving a Fox-Pach-Suk conjecture on clique/stable-set size in graphs of bounded VC-dimension). It has no connection to hypergraph Ramsey numbers r(K_s^{(3)}, K_n^{(3)}), and its fuzz=70 match was a false positive. The paper_contribution text in the internal reference describes a completely different conjecture.

search_queries: off-diagonal hypergraph Ramsey numbers r(K_s^3 K_n^3) lower bound 2^n^c arXiv 2404.02021
Conlon Fox Gunby He Mubayi Suk Verstraete hypergraph Ramsey 2024 conjecture polynomial exponential
notes: Two related follow-up papers were found but do not resolve Conjecture 4.6: arXiv:2411.13812 ('When are off-diagonal hypergraph Ramsey numbers polynomial?', 2024) characterizes polynomial growth for general H, the complementary direction; arXiv:2603.16069 ('Hypergraph Ramsey numbers with quasipolynomial growth rate', Xiaoyu He, 2026) constructs specific 3-graphs achieving n^{Theta(log n)} Ramsey numbers, demonstrating intermediate growth rates but for non-complete hypergraphs. Neither paper addresses the complete hypergraph K_s^{(3)} setting of Conjecture 4.6. The conjecture is closely related to the stepping-up paradigm (Erdős-Hajnal-Rado) applied to complete hypergraphs, but no explicit resolution via that approach was located in the indexed literature.
